title: Source Registration, Subscriptions, Following, and Ownership · Go Semantics Case: Feed Go Source Registration
summary: Each user creates duplicate fetch jobs for the same feed, unfollow deletes shared data, ownership is ambiguous, and source verification happens outside a transaction. Predict the reader decision, feed or API contract, Go and PostgreSQL ownership, bounded failure, and observable evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the feed go source registration increment in shared-source and tenant subscription model. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theo…
objectives: Register a discovered source idempotently from normalized identity, provenance, scope decision, and initial fetch evidence.
Classify constraint, serialization, deadlock, cancellation, timeout, connection, and unknown failures without leaking SQL or secrets.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and Go Predict Feed Go Source Registration · Theory Feed Go Source Registration

theory case — A theory Go team handles case 3139 during a PostgreSQL claim and failover drill. Build deterministic Go evidence to reconcile shared source identity, tenant subscriptions, lifecycle, authorization, and registration races; failed, canceled, stale, and cross-tenant operations must preserve the prior admitted state; then change one tenant, cursor, reader-state, authorization, cache, or accessibility condition without leaking state. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.
